_meta.update_note: Incremental update — BUG-TOGGLE-002/003/004/005 + ExpandedView refactor + BUG-ITER-STOP. NEW: ExpandedView.jsx (orchestrator, 92 lines), ExpandedTransportBar.jsx (217), ExpandedHistoryList.jsx (359), ExpandedDetailPanel.jsx (496), promptUtils.js. DELETED: ExpandedIdleView.jsx. INFERRED→EXTRACTED: WaveformCanvas←RecordingState, MorphCanvas←ThinkingState. App.jsx now imports ExpandedView. main.js gains hide-on-blur. God node: App.jsx at 21 connections (SRP flag).

god_nodes.srp_note: 20 connections — SRP violation likely; consider further extraction

package.json.concept_evidence: Core project manifest — no feature folder owns it

imported_by.evidence: electron-builder config in package.json references entitlements.plist for hardened runtime

rationale.text: hardenedRuntime: true + gatekeeperAssess: false added (BUG-015/FEATURE-SIGNING) — hardenedRuntime is required for the self-signed cert approach to grant persistent TCC entry; gatekeeperAssess: false skips Gatekeeper check for unsigned builds so dev installs work
dist:unsigned script added — runs electron-builder without signing; useful for CI/dev when no cert is set up; dist:signed (FEATURE-SIGNING) orchestrates create-cert + electron-builder + sign-app

rationale.note: v1.4.0 — added release script, signIgnore for uninstall.sh

entitlements.plist.concept_evidence: macOS distribution entitlement — no feature folder owns it

imported_by.evidence: electron-builder hardenedRuntime + entitlements config references this file

rationale.text: com.apple.security.network.client added (BUG-015) — required for hardenedRuntime: true; without it, any outbound network call (even to localhost) is blocked by the hardened sandbox

eslint.config.js.concept_evidence: ESLint 9 flat config — dev tooling, no feature ownership

vite.config.js.concept_evidence: Vite build config — root: src/renderer, outDir: dist-renderer/, plugins: react() + tailwindcss()

rationale.text: base: './' — dist-renderer loaded via file:// protocol by Electron; absolute paths would break asset loading

main.js.concept_evidence: Electron main process — owns IPC hub, window lifecycle, PATH resolution; touched by multiple features but no single feature owns it
main.js.secondary_concept_evidence: transcribe-audio IPC (speech-recording), generate-prompt + show-mode-menu IPC (claude-integration), splash-* IPC channels (first-run), get-theme + nativeTheme.on (dark-mode), createTray/updateTrayMenu (tray), resize-window-width + set-window-size + show-history (history-panel), save-file (export), generate-raw IPC (iteration-mode), request-mic + check-mic-status + session.defaultSession handler (signing/mic permission), remove DevTools (ui-polish), set-last-prompt IPC + lastGeneratedPrompt + copy-last-prompt tray item (quick-copy)

imports.evidence: webPreferences.preload for both win and splashWin

loads.evidence: win.loadFile('dist-renderer/index.html') in production; win.loadURL('http://localhost:5173') in dev

rationale.text: PATH resolution uses 'zsh -lc "which claude"' — claude binary lives in shell PATH set by .zshrc/.zprofile; plain exec('claude') would miss it in Electron's restricted environment
transcript passed via stdin to claude spawn (not shell arg) — avoids shell injection and arg length limits
rationale.evidence: child.stdin.end() with systemPrompt containing transcript embedded via PROMPT_TEMPLATE

rationale.text: nativeTheme.on('updated') fires theme-changed IPC to renderer; get-theme handler returns initial state at boot — renderer needs both: initial value on load + live updates
app.dock.hide() called in createTray() — app lives in menu bar only; dock entry would be confusing for a persistent floating tool

BrowserWindow has minWidth:520, maxWidth:520 — overridden at runtime by set-window-size IPC which calls setMinimumSize/setMaximumSize before setSize. Any resize that needs a non-520 width must use set-window-size, not resize-window-width alone.

set-window-size IPC added (BUG-011) — separate width+height calls had a race condition: resize-window (RAF-deferred) read win.getSize() before resize-window-width had applied, clamping width back to 520
resolveWhisperPath() added (BUG-012) — whisper binary resolves via 'zsh -lc "which whisper"' then symlink-followed to real binary; pyenv/conda shims would resolve to wrong env otherwise

session.defaultSession.setPermissionRequestHandler grants mic automatically (BUG-013) — prevents repeated OS dialog in packaged builds; TCC entry is seeded once in splash via getUserMedia, then Electron's handler bypasses subsequent native prompts

isDestroyed() guards on every win/splashWin access (BUG-015) — splash-done timeout extended to 1200ms; without guards, async callbacks racing window destruction threw 'Object has been destroyed' errors

check-mic-status IPC returns systemPreferences.getMediaAccessStatus('microphone') — lets splash check TCC status before deciding whether to call getUserMedia; avoids redundant mic request if already granted

buildTrayMenu() refactor (FEATURE-018/QCPY) — shared helper called by both createTray() and updateTrayMenu(), prevents drift between initial and updated tray menus. lastGeneratedPrompt is session-only (not persisted) — set via set-last-prompt IPC after each successful generate-prompt. copy-last-prompt tray item copies silently without opening window.

rationale.note: win.on("blur") hides window (fix/window commit, 63fa100) — auto-dismiss on focus loss. isExpanded guard not needed: blur fires after user switches away.

preload.js.concept_evidence: contextBridge IPC bridge — shared by all renderer windows, no single feature owns it

imported_by.evidence: webPreferences.preload for both win and splashWin

window.electronAPI.* consumed throughout — resizeWindow, setWindowSize, generatePrompt, transcribeAudio, copyToClipboard, onShortcutTriggered, onShowHistory, onShowShortcuts, setWindowButtonsVisible, getTheme, onThemeChanged, showModeMenu, saveFile, setLastPrompt

removed_methods.reason: BUG-015 — App.jsx no longer calls requestMic(); splash.html calls getUserMedia() directly; TCC grant handled once at splash time via getUserMedia, not repeated per-recording

rationale.text: nodeIntegration: false + contextIsolation: true always — security boundary between renderer and Node.js; all IPC via window.electronAPI only
rationale.evidence: contextBridge pattern enforces this; ARCHITECTURE.md rule

rationale.text: onShortcutPause added (FEATURE-011) — shortcut-pause IPC channel existed since FSC-004 (Alt+P stub) but was never wired to renderer via contextBridge until pause/resume was implemented

generateRaw added (FEATURE-012) — separate from generatePrompt; takes { systemPrompt } directly (no PROMPT_TEMPLATE wrapper) to support iteration's custom combined prompt; IPC channel is 'generate-raw'

requestMic removed (BUG-015) — App.jsx was calling requestMic() at startRecording, causing repeated mic dialogs on every recording. TCC grant is now a one-time splash responsibility via getUserMedia. preload no longer exposes this method.

splash.html.concept_evidence: D-007 decision: FIRST_RUN state removed from index.html, replaced by separate splashWin BrowserWindow loading splash.html

imports.evidence: window.electronAPI consumed for splashDone, splashCheckCLI, splashOpenURL, requestMic IPC calls

rationale.text: getUserMedia() called directly in splash.html renderer (BUG-013) — seeds the TCC microphone grant once per app install; session.defaultSession permission handler in main.js then auto-grants subsequent requests so recordings never trigger a repeated dialog

check-mic-status IPC checked before getUserMedia (BUG-016) — if TCC status is already 'granted', splash skips the getUserMedia call entirely; avoids redundant mic UI flash on every launch after first use

index.html.concept_evidence: Legacy vanilla JS renderer — stays on main branch as reference; replaced by React build (dist-renderer/) on live app
index.html.review_note: index.html is the legacy monolithic UI file. The live app loads dist-renderer/index.html (built from src/renderer/). This file is kept for reference only.

rationale.text: index.html retained on main branch as legacy reference after React migration (FEATURE-004). Not loaded by Electron in production — main.js loads dist-renderer/index.html instead.

src/renderer/index.css.concept_evidence: Tailwind v4 entry — @import 'tailwindcss', @theme tokens, @keyframes, body reset

rationale.text: pauseGlow @keyframes added (FEATURE-011) — amber glow animation for pause/resume buttons; inline animation: property references this keyframe name directly since Tailwind @theme token generates [animation:pauseGlow_2s_ease-in-out_infinite] class
iterGlow @keyframes added (FEATURE-012) — violet/indigo glow animation for IteratingState; IteratingState.jsx references it inline as animation:'iterGlow 2s ease-in-out infinite' (no Tailwind class)

POLISH-001–009 — state transitions added (opacity + translateY), font-weight tuning, hover states on all interactive elements, pulse ring enhancements, custom scrollbar styles for HistoryPanel, text brightness calibration; all via Tailwind utility classes or CSS tokens

rationale.note: BUG-TOGGLE-004/005: pulse-ring @keyframes (scale 1→1.8), breathe (scale 0.97→1.03), spin, skeleton-pulse added. Referenced inline by ExpandedView components. RFX-EXP-005 moved these keyframes from ExpandedView.jsx to index.css to enable cross-component sharing.

src/renderer/App.jsx.concept_evidence: Root state machine — owns STATES, STATE_HEIGHTS, transition(), all IPC listeners, recording flow, theme wiring

rationale.note: Central state machine — transition() is the only function that mutates UI state
BUG-033: recording + keyboard concerns extracted to useRecording.js + useKeyboardShortcuts.js but App.jsx still imports 18 files — god node status worsened as features were added
stateRef mirrors currentState for stale-closure-safe IPC callbacks — IPC handlers registered once at mount can't see useState updates

isExpanded (useState bool) added for expanded-view toggle — App renders ExpandedView for ALL states when isExpanded=true, bypassing individual state components. openHistory/closeHistory/openSettings call setWindowSize directly, bypassing transition() to allow non-standard window width.

imported_by.evidence: import { getHistory, deleteHistoryItem, clearHistory, searchHistory, formatTime } line 2

src/renderer/utils/history.js.storage_entry_shape: { id, title, transcript, prompt, mode, timestamp, isIteration?, basedOn?, polishChanges?, bookmarked?, rating?, ratingTag? }

rationale.text: All localStorage history access gated through this module — ARCHITECTURE.md rule: no direct localStorage.* calls outside wrapper functions
getHistory() wraps JSON.parse in try/catch returning [] — corrupted or missing key is a silent no-op, not a crash

saveToHistory() accepts optional { isIteration, basedOn } fields (FEATURE-012) — isIteration:true marks entry for HistoryPanel badge; basedOn stores first 100 chars of original prompt for provenance display

bookmarkHistoryItem() toggles bookmarked boolean and returns new value — toggle-pattern matches existing delete approach (mutate array, re-persist). rateHistoryItem() sets rating ('up'|'down'|null) + ratingTag on same entry. Both added for FEATURE-020 (history-v2).
Full inline styles — no Tailwind classes. Tailwind layout classes were not applying in HISTORY state (purge/JIT issue). Inline styles are immune and explicit.
Right panel has minHeight:0 on the flex container — without it, flex items don't shrink below content size and overflowY:auto on the inner prompt div never activates
↻ iterated badge shown on history entry when entry.isIteration === true (FEATURE-012) — distinguishes AI-refined iterations from original recordings in the history list

src/renderer/hooks/useMode.js.concept_evidence: Wraps localStorage mode key — directly supports mode selection feature

src/renderer/hooks/useWindowResize.js.concept_evidence: Wraps resizeWindow IPC — shared infrastructure, no single feature owns it

rationale.text: resizeWindow wrapped in requestAnimationFrame — defers IPC call until after React render to avoid visual flash during state transition
'built using vibe-* skills' footer added — attribution line; renders below the ⌘? hint chip in IDLE state

rationale.note: Expand button (⤢) added top-right corner — transitions App.jsx isExpanded to true. BUG-TOGGLE-003: breathing ring as separate div (border + breathe 3s keyframe), mic icon rgba(255,255,255,0.55).

rationale.text: Internal timer (useState + setInterval) removed in FEATURE-011 — duration now flows down as prop from App.jsx; required for timer to survive RECORDING→PAUSED→RECORDING transitions without resetting to 0

src/renderer/components/PausedState.jsx.concept_evidence: vibe/features/2026-04-20-pause-resume/ — PAUZ-002 Touches field; sole purpose is to render PAUSED state UI

rationale.text: Flat amber gradient line instead of WaveformCanvas — waveform animation would need to be frozen-in-place; a static ambient line communicates 'paused' more clearly with zero complexity
Stop button retained in PAUSED state — MediaRecorder.stop() works natively from 'paused' state; user should not need to resume before stopping

imported_by.evidence: import WaveformCanvas line 1 — confirmed in source
import WaveformCanvas line 1 — waveform zone during RECORDING in expanded view

rationale.note: BUG-TOGGLE-004: DPR-aware sizing added — canvas.width = offsetWidth * devicePixelRatio, ctx.scale(dpr, dpr). Glow layer lineWidth 3 at rgba(200,50,35,0.07), sharp line lineWidth 1 with red gradient. Fixes blurry canvas on Retina displays.

imports.evidence: confirmed by source grep — import MorphCanvas

imported_by.evidence: import MorphCanvas line 2 — morph wave in THINKING + ITERATING in expanded transport bar

rationale.note: BUG-TOGGLE-004: DPR-aware sizing added. glow lineWidth 3, sharp line lineWidth 1, amplitude max ~4px, blue gradient peak 0.4 opacity. Consistent with WaveformCanvas DPR fix applied in same session.

src/renderer/components/IteratingState.j.concept_evidence: vibe/features/2026-04-20-iteration-mode/ — ITR-002 Touches field; sole purpose is ITERATING state UI

rationale.text: All styles inline — no Tailwind classes, matching HistoryPanel.jsx pattern; avoids Tailwind JIT purge issues for rarely-activated ITERATING state

Uses iterGlow @keyframes from index.css inline — violet/indigo colour distinguishes ITERATING from RECORDING (blue waveform) and PAUSED (amber); visual hierarchy: new input=violet, prev recording=blue

scripts/create-cert.sh.concept_evidence: FEATURE-SIGNING — creates a self-signed code signing certificate in the macOS Keychain for persistent TCC mic grant

rationale.text: Legacy PKCS12 fallback included — security import -f pkcs12 works on older macOS where newer API unavailable; tmpdir used for intermediate files and cleaned up on exit

scripts/sign-app.sh.concept_evidence: FEATURE-SIGNING — codesigns dist/mac-universal/Promptly.app with self-signed cert + entitlements.plist

scripts/build-signed.sh.concept_evidence: FEATURE-SIGNING — orchestration script: create-cert → npm run dist → sign-app; maps to 'dist:signed' npm script

scripts/generate-icon.js.concept_evidence: Build tooling — generates build/icon.icns from build/icon.png; one-time dev utility

rationale.note: Extracted from App.jsx by BUG-033 to resolve SRP violation — recording state (mediaRecorderRef, chunksRef, recSecsRef, isProcessingRef) + callbacks delegated here
Extracted from App.jsx by BUG-033 — IPC listeners (onShowHistory, onOpenSettings, onShowShortcuts) + keydown handler delegated here to keep App.jsx focused on state machine
localStorage wrapper for promptly_polish_tone — never access localStorage directly per CLAUDE.md rule
Encapsulates polish mode state: polishResult, polishTone, parsePolishOutput — extracted to keep App.jsx SRP
Separate component for polish output — different layout (polished text + changes section) than PromptReadyState; green accent throughout
Controlled textarea for typed prompts — resizeWindow prop passed from App.jsx (not hook) per FEATURE-014 convention; dynamic height on content

src/renderer/utils/promptUtils.js.concept_evidence: Extracted from ExpandedView.jsx refactor (RFX-EXP-004) — shared render utilities used only by Expanded components

rationale.note: Extracted from ExpandedView.jsx (RFX-EXP-004) to eliminate duplication — parseSections used by ExpandedDetailPanel, getModeTagStyle used by ExpandedHistoryList; both are pure functions with no component dependency

src/renderer/components/ExpandedView.jsx.concept_evidence: Primary orchestrator for 1100×860 expanded layout — BUG-TOGGLE-002 through BUG-TOGGLE-005

rationale.note: Thin orchestrator (92 lines) — owns only selected + isViewingHistory state, delegates layout to three sub-components. BUG-TOGGLE-002 tore down ExpandedIdleView.jsx (wrong generic mic screen) and built this from scratch.

Window: 1100×860 (Claude app dimensions, BUG-TOGGLE-005). Three zones: ExpandedTransportBar (top), ExpandedHistoryList (left 300px), ExpandedDetailPanel (right flex:1). All proportions scaled at BUG-TOGGLE-005.

src/renderer/components/ExpandedTranspor.concept_evidence: Extracted from ExpandedView.jsx refactor (RFX-EXP-001) — top transport row of expanded layout

rationale.note: BUG-ITER-STOP fix lives here — ITERATING check was missing, centre button called onStart instead of onStopIterate. Fixed by adding onStopIterate prop chain. Blue stop + blue pulse rings + MorphCanvas waveform during ITERATING.

src/renderer/components/ExpandedHistoryL.concept_evidence: Extracted from ExpandedView.jsx refactor (RFX-EXP-002) — left panel of expanded layout, 359 lines

imports.evidence: import { getHistory, deleteHistoryItem, clearHistory, searchHistory } line 2

rationale.note: Owns its own history/filter state (search, tabs, chips, stats). Syncs bookmark/rating display from selected prop passed from parent. Full parity with HistoryPanel.jsx — search, All/Saved tabs, filter chips, stats bar, ↻ badge, clear all.

src/renderer/components/ExpandedDetailPa.concept_evidence: Extracted from ExpandedView.jsx refactor (RFX-EXP-003) — right detail panel, 496 lines

rationale.note: Right panel dual-mode: shows history entry detail when isViewingHistory=true; shows per-state content (IDLE placeholder, RECORDING progress, THINKING, PROMPT_READY actions) otherwise.

src/renderer/components/ExpandedIdleView.deletion_reason: BUG-TOGGLE-002 — tore down as wrong generic mic screen. Replaced by full ExpandedView.jsx three-zone layout.
